A faint click echoes from a ledge above just before a hand crossbow bolt sparks off stone. The attacker moves before the sound dies.
A roar shakes loose dust from the tunnel roof as a white-furred brute pounds forward. It has marked the cavern with claw scars and bone piles.
An ore cart rolls out of the dark with no one visible pushing it. The duergar appear only when the party steps near the tracks.
Glowing fungus dots the ceiling in patterns that mimic unfamiliar stars. Reading the pattern reveals a route through the lightless caverns.
A distant boom rolls through the cave and nearby puffballs burst in reply. A quick breath keeps the spores from taking hold.
A narrow bridge of black crystal crosses a silent chasm. The edges are sharp enough to cut through gloves when the footing slips.
Violet moss hides a line of old runes carved into a tunnel wall. Someone recently scraped one clean, then fled in a hurry.
A cracked iron bell lies in the passage, ringing softly whenever no one looks at it. Its missing chain points toward a hidden camp.
Two rival sets of tracks circle a valuable mushroom grove. Settling the claim may prevent a small underground war.
Bluecaps grow in a damp ring around a stalagmite, edible if harvested before they shed bitter spores. The smallest caps are safest.
Layered fungus grows like bark along a warm cavern wall. Thin strips dry into chewy rations if cut cleanly.
A mat of glowmoss carpets the base of a stone pillar. Gathering it without smothering the remaining patch requires careful scraping.
Pale fish drift through a still black lake, guided by tremors more than sight. The wrong splash sends them into deep cracks.
Tiny shrimp swarm around a warm mineral seep, turning the water pink. A fine net can collect them before the current changes.
Black eels knot beneath flat stones in a cold stream. Their slick bodies are hard to grip and harder to keep.
A pale glimmer threads through dark stone, too small for a rich strike but too rare to ignore. The trace must be chased gently.
A seam of black iron runs beside old pick scars. Working it wakes echoes that travel too far down the tunnel.
A cracked geode shows purple points behind a thin limestone skin. The crystals can be freed if the pocket is opened slowly.
Tall zurkhwood stalks grow near a phosphorescent pool, woody enough for boards when dried. Cutting them too low poisons the root bed.
A dead shelf fungus has hardened into long rib-like beams. They are light, strong, and brittle at the ends.
Old mine braces stand in a dead-end shaft that no longer bears weight. Salvaging the right pieces avoids touching the live supports.
White silk marks a bridge over a black crevasse, beautiful and too clean. Drow scouts wait below the lip where torchlight cannot reach.
A gray iron gate blocks the tunnel, worked into the stone so recently that chips still glitter. Invisible guards demand a toll in whispers.
Eggshell crunches underfoot before the first hook scrapes stone overhead. The rookery answers disturbance with shrieks from every tunnel.
Tiny chalk marks hide under ledges where tall folk seldom look. Reading them reveals a svirfneblin safe path through unstable stone.
Violet sparks crawl over the cavern walls and twist familiar sounds into nonsense. Holding focus keeps the surge from scrambling thought.
Bell-shaped fungi ring softly as air moves through the cavern. The pattern resembles a warning repeated in three languages.
A deep gnome's pickaxe lies beside a tunnel too narrow for armored pursuers. The owner scratched a plea for silence into the haft.
A dropped iron ledger lists tolls collected from travelers who never returned. One entry is scheduled for tomorrow and names the party's route.
A myconid circle offers a shared dream of a tunnel collapse before it happens. The dream includes a face none of them recognize.
A voice answers from a wall only after the party speaks into a different tunnel. The echo knows where a hidden cell was carved.
A crystal spider carries a message cylinder through the dark, avoiding light and footsteps. Catching it reveals a raid order for a surface road.
Bluecaps grow in a damp hollow lit by faerzress. The edible caps hum faintly and bruise black under rough hands.
A sheet of thick lichen peels from warm stone like flatbread. It is bland, filling, and easy to spoil with grit.
Glowspores cling beneath a ledge, useful for lamps and dyes. A slow cut keeps them from bursting into choking dust.
Blind eels coil in an underground lake where the shore drops away sharply. They follow vibrations faster than hooks.
Transparent shrimp shimmer around a warm mineral vent. Their shells glow for minutes after leaving the water.
Flat black fish cling to the bottom of a slow river. A weighted net can lift them before the current steals it.
A hair-thin mithral glint runs through dark stone. It may be only a trace, but even traces are worth careful work.
Long crystals grow from a cavern ceiling and chime when touched. Cutting one without starting a cascade is delicate work.
Heavy dark iron nodules stud a wall beside old duergar tool marks. They come free only after the stone is warmed.
A zurkhwood stalk rises thick and pale near a fungal grove. The mature sections cut like wood and dry into strong planks.
Ancient scaffold beams cross a mining shaft and creak under their own age. Some are still sound if removed in the right order.
Rope-like fungal roots hang from the ceiling in dry loops. Cut lengths twist into sturdy cord once smoked.
